raw.objective: “to characterize the steady-state pharmacokinetics of gabapentin in the target population” (paediatric population)
raw.covariates: WT, Population (healthy/patient) on Vd/F. Race, CrCL on CL/F
raw.observations: Residual variability smaller in subjects than patients. Some bias in predicting lower concentrations in healthy subjects. No saturable absorption taken into account.
raw.dose: Steady-state oral dosing in healthy subjects and epileptic patients
raw.structure: 1 compartment model with first order absorption with lag time and first order elimination
raw.equations: CL/F=theta1*CrCL*(1+theta2*RACE)*exp(eta1) Vd/F=(theta3+theta4*WT)*(1+theta5*POP)*exp(eta2) Ka=theta6*exp(eta3) ALAG1=theta7*exp(eta4) Cobs=POP*(Cipred*(1+eps1)+eps2) + (1-POP)*(Cipred*(1+eps3)+eps4) RACE: 1 for black, 0 for other POP: 1 for patients, 0 for healthy subjects
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.116 theta2=0.357 theta3=3.26 theta4=1.34 theta5=1.05 theta6=1.24 theta7=0.181 omega1=0.0882 omega2=0.0936 omega3=0.185 omega4=0.626 sigma1=0.00956 sigma2=0.0625 sigma3=0.0640 sigma4=1.74

structural_model.description: 1 compartment model with first order absorption with lag time and first order elimination

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=theta1*CrCL*(1+theta2*RACE)*exp(eta1) Vd/F=(theta3+theta4*WT)*(1+theta5*POP)*exp(eta2) Ka=theta6*exp(eta3) ALAG1=theta7*exp(eta4) Cobs=POP*(Cipred*(1+eps1)+eps2) + (1-POP)*(Cipred*(1+eps3)+eps4) RACE: 1 for black, 0 for other POP: 1 for patients, 0 for healthy subjects
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady-state oral dosing in healthy subjects and epileptic patients
study.objective: “to characterize the steady-state pharmacokinetics of gabapentin in the target population” (paediatric population)

covariates.raw: WT, Population (healthy/patient) on Vd/F. Race, CrCL on CL/F

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
